expectations.text: correct_feature_ids — All feature IDs match F-X.Y.Z format
correct_requirement_ids — All requirement IDs match R-X.Y.Z or R-X.Y.Za/b/c format
correct_story_ids — All user story IDs match US-X.Y.Z format
expectations.evidence: User story IDs use US-X.Y.Z.W format (4-level) instead of US-X.Y.Z: e.g., US-6.4.6.1, US-6.4.6.2, US-15.21.13.1, US-15.21.14.1, etc.

expectations.text: correct_domain — IDs use an appropriate module number for the topic
expectations.evidence: Runtime haptic features use domain 6.4 (Haptics) and editor features use 15.21 (Specialized Editors), both appropriate for haptic feedback authoring

expectations.text: one_to_many_requirements — At least some features have 2+ requirements
expectations.evidence: Multiple features have 2 requirements: F-6.4.6 has R-6.4.6 and R-6.4.6a, F-15.21.13 has R-15.21.13 and R-15.21.13a, F-15.21.14 has R-15.21.14 and R-15.21.14a, F-15.21.16 has R-15.21.16 and R-15.21.16a

expectations.text: nfr_suffixes — Non-functional requirements use letter suffixes (a, b, c)
expectations.evidence: NFRs use 'a' suffix: R-6.4.6a (mixer performance), R-15.21.13a (editor frame rate), R-15.21.14a (preview latency), R-15.21.16a (simulation accuracy)

expectations.text: one_to_many_stories — At least some features have 2+ user stories from different personas
expectations.evidence: Multiple features have 3+ stories: F-6.4.6 has 4 stories (designer P-5, engine developer P-26, player P-23, engine tester P-27); F-15.21.13 has 5 stories from 4 personas

expectations.text: varied_personas — User stories reference at least 3 distinct personas
expectations.evidence: 7 distinct personas: designer (P-5), engine developer (P-26), player (P-23), engine tester (P-27), audio designer (P-14), QA tester (P-19), prototyper (P-7)

expectations.text: shall_in_requirements — Requirements use SHALL or SHOULD language
expectations.evidence: All 11 requirements use SHALL: e.g., R-6.4.6 'The engine SHALL mix multiple concurrent haptic effects', R-15.21.13 'The editor SHALL provide a visual timeline'

expectations.text: no_shall_in_features — Feature file does not contain SHALL
expectations.evidence: features.md contains no instances of SHALL. Uses descriptive language: 'Mix multiple simultaneous haptic effects', 'A visual timeline editor', 'Real-time haptic preview'

expectations.text: rationale_and_verification — Requirements include Rationale and Verification sections
expectations.evidence: All 11 requirements include Rationale and Verification subsections with specific test procedures

expectations.text: acceptance_criteria — User stories include Acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: All user stories include Acceptance criteria, e.g., US-6.4.6.1: 'Two effects on different channels play simultaneously. An effect with higher priority overrides a lower-priority effect on the same channel'

expectations.text: feature_expansions — Output includes features beyond the literal idea (suggested additions)
expectations.evidence: Features go beyond basic editor authoring to include: F-6.4.6 (Haptic Channel Mixer — runtime mixing), F-6.4.7 (Hot Reload), F-15.21.15 (Template Library), F-15.21.16 (Per-Platform Testing), F-15.21.17 (Debug Overlay)

expectations.text: cross_domain_deps — Features reference dependencies on existing features from other domains
expectations.evidence: Cross-domain deps: F-6.4.6 depends on F-1.1.1 (ECS), F-6.4.7 depends on F-12.4.4 (content pipeline hot-reload), F-15.21.13 depends on F-15.1.3 (editor undo/redo), F-15.21.14 depends on F-6.1.5, F-15.21.17 depends on F-15.5.6 (Stat Overlays)

expectations.text: user_interaction_planned — (with_skill only) user-questions.md exists with review questions for both feature confirmation and requirements/stories review
expectations.evidence: user-questions.md exists with 'Phase 2 Questions — Feature Confirmation' (5 questions about feature selection, expansions, missing features, scope) and 'Phase 4 Questions — Requirements and Stories Review' (5 questions about performance targets, persona coverage, NFRs)
